what_it_does: Reads a manifest of file paths, sends the first 8 KB of each file to a local OpenAI-compatible chat-completions endpoint with a classification prompt, and appends JSONL classification records to an output file while resuming from any prior output.
observations: Hardcodes ROOT, MANIFEST, OUT, URL, and MODEL paths/values.
Uses urllib.request to call http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions with response_format set to json_object.
The system prompt instructs the model to classify by kind, domain, role, and built status and to return strict JSON only.
The classify function retries up to 3 times on failure with 1-second sleeps.
Reads only the first 8000 bytes of each file and ignores encoding errors.
Resumes by collecting already-processed rel values from existing output.
Runs 24 concurrent workers via ThreadPoolExecutor and appends results line-by-line with periodic progress logging.
standouts: Local LLM-based batch classifier for project files.
Resume logic from partial JSONL output.
High concurrency (24 workers) against a local endpoint.
conventions: Writes incremental JSONL with flush and periodic progress logging.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths and model name reduce portability.
No rate limiting or backoff beyond a fixed 1-second retry sleep.
Truncates files at 8000 bytes, which may misclassify files whose relevant content appears later.
Uses errors='ignore' when reading files, which can silently corrupt input context.
Does not validate the schema of the LLM response beyond json.loads.
notes: A quick, resume-capable script that uses a local chat model to classify project files in bulk.
questions: Is the local endpoint expected to handle 24 concurrent requests reliably?
Why is the file read capped at 8000 bytes rather than a token budget?
How are classification errors intended to be reconciled or reprocessed?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does what its content suggests: bulk classification of project files via a local LLM API, with resume support.
intention_signals: The author seems to be trying to automatically tag a large set of project files by kind, domain, role, and build status using a local language model, while avoiding reprocessing already-scanned files.
contribution: Provides a concrete, runnable pattern for resume-capable, concurrent LLM-based classification of a file manifest into a JSONL ledger.
summary_for_embedding: scan.py is a Python script that reads a manifest of project files, classifies each by kind/domain/role/built status using a local chat-completions endpoint, and appends JSONL results with resume support.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to bootstrap a classification pass over a project directory so each file could be tagged for later migration or cataloging.

symbols.does: Reads the first 8 KB of a file, sends its path and content to a local chat-completions endpoint with a classification prompt, and returns a JSON classification record or an error record.
symbols.description: The classify function builds a request body containing a system prompt that demands strict JSON output with kind, domain, role, and built fields, plus a user message with the relative path and file head. It posts to the configured local URL up to three times, parses the returned JSON, attaches the relative path, and returns the record; on persistent failure it returns an object with the relative path and a truncated error string.
